title: Intensely Tangy Kenyan Smoked Mackerel Skewers
description: A show-off Kenyan dish featuring smoked mackerel, with a tangy and cheese-pull flavor profile, perfect for special occasions, and friendly to low-glycemic diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, ginger, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Add the smoked mackerel pieces to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Thread the marinated mackerel pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 12 minutes.
While the mackerel is baking, heat about 2-3 inches of oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the mackerel from the oven and carefully place the skewers in the hot oil, frying for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Remove the skewers from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese over the mackerel and place under the broiler for 1-2 minutes, until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Serve the intensely tangy Kenyan smoked mackerel skewers hot, garnished with sliced green chili peppers.
